dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires short_window=30 and long_window=120. Mapped to closest available SMAs: sma_40 (30-period proxy) and sma_100 (120-period proxy). Used 'crosses_above' and 'crosses_below' operators to detect the crossover events. Entry requires both crossover confirmation and price level confirmation (sma_40 > sma_100). Position state conditions (position_state == OUT/LONG) are handled by the backtest engine's position management, not in the rule itself.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires short_window=30 and long_window=120. Mapped to closest available SMAs: sma_40 (30-period proxy) and sma_100 (120-period proxy). Used 'crosses_above' and 'crosses_below' operators to detect the crossover events. Entry requires both crossover confirmation and price level confirmation (sma_40 > sma_100). Position state conditions (position_state == OUT/LONG) are handled by the backtest engine's position management, not in the rule itself.

source_rule_text: Generate a long entry signal when the short-term moving average crosses above the long-term moving average (bullish crossover), and generate an exit signal when the short-term moving average crosses below the long-term moving average (bearish crossover).
